mnemonics.title: B+ Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: B+ TREE: Balanced, Trees (hierarchical), leaf-nodes contain data, Overflow handled by splitting, all Records accessible via leaves, Equal-height search paths

mnemonics.title: Index Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content:
CLUSTERED: Physical ORDER matters, Only ONE per table
NON-CLUSTERED: NOn-physical order, Many can exist

mnemonics.title: Dense vs Sparse
mnemonics.content:
DENSE: Every record DIsplayed in index, More space, Faster find
SPARSE: Some records SPared from index, Less space, Slower find

acronyms.usage: A file organization technique combining sequential storage with multi-level indexing for efficient retrieval
Software that manages database operations including file organization and indexing
Person responsible for designing file organizations and index strategies
Storage technology that can affect file organization performance

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree with root at top, branches spreading down, all leaf nodes at same bottom level in a horizontal row, connected by horizontal lines showing linked list
Clustered = book with pages physically sorted by topic; Non-clustered = book index at back with page numbers pointing to scattered content
Think of a library: main index (floor guide) → section index (aisle markers) → actual books
Think of lockers numbered 0-9, key value 'student ID' hashed to get locker number, multiple students may share locker (collision)

analogies.analogy: Think of a library: books can be arranged by subject (clustered), by author name (indexed), or just thrown in a pile (heap) - each has different access speeds
Like a book's table of contents or index at the back - it tells you exactly where to find information without reading every page
Like a company's org chart: CEO (root) → Managers (intermediate) → Workers (leaves). Everyone reports to someone above, but only workers do the actual work
Like a hotel room key card system: room number (key) is run through algorithm to tell you exactly which floor and room, no searching required
